description:
The quiz evaluates a developer's knowledge of advanced HTML5 semantic elements beyond basic tags like <div> and <span>, measuring both conceptual understanding and practical usage. It is positioned as a moderate‑difficulty, light‑hearted challenge with a friendly, encouraging tone that nudges learners to improve their markup skills. The format mixes code snippets with informal commentary to keep the experience engaging.
Topics: HTML5 semantic elements, Proper use of <header>, <nav>, <section>, <article>, <footer>, etc., Accessibility and SEO implications of semantic markup, Differences between generic and semantic tags
Audience: Front‑end web developers and web design students who are comfortable with basic HTML and want to validate or deepen their semantic HTML knowledge.
